research_areas: Sign systems — how signs constitute meaning in natural and artificial contexts
Peirce triadic model — icon, index, symbol and the semiotic triangle
Indexicality — signs that point to their referents through causal or existential connection
Denotation and connotation — first-order and second-order meaning
Semiosphere — Lotman's universe of sign systems and cultural boundaries
Code theory — natural, cultural, technical, and computational codes
Agent communication — how AI agents interpret instructions and negotiate meaning
Interpretation theory — Peirce's unlimited semiosis and hermeneutic frameworks
curriculum: Saussurean sign — signifier, signified, arbitrariness, linearity
Peircean semiotics — icon, index, symbol; semiotic triangle; unlimited semiosis
Barthes and myth — denotation, connotation, mythologization
Code theory — Eco's notion of codes, subcodes, and overcoding
Semiosphere and cultural semiotics — Lotman's boundary dynamics
Narrative semiotics — Greimas's actantial model and semantic square
Pragmatics and indexicality — context, deixis, ostension
Computational semiotics — machine interpretation, protocol semantics, meaning negotiation
Galactic Protocol semiotic analysis — how governance contracts carry meaning
Semiotics of music — topoi, gestures, metaphor, and embodied meaning
